title: Atraumatic tips for prosthetic implant delivery apparatus
abstract: A delivery apparatus includes a dock sleeve having a body portion and a tip portion connected to a distal end of the body portion. The body portion is configured to retain a docking device for a prosthetic implant. The tip portion includes a wall and at least one slit cutting through a thickness of the wall. The tip portion has a cylindrical shape when no compressive load is applied to a distal end of the tip portion. The at least one slit causes the tip portion to move from the cylindrical shape to a deformed shape when pressing the distal end of the tip portion against an external object such that the distal end of the tip portion deflects from the external object.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1. A delivery apparatus comprising: a dock sleeve comprising a body portion and a tip portion connected to a distal end of the body portion, wherein the body portion is configured to retain a docking device for a prosthetic implant, wherein the tip portion comprises a wall and at least one slit cutting through a thickness of the wall, wherein the tip portion has a cylindrical shape when no compressive load is applied to a distal end of the tip portion, and wherein the at least one slit causes the tip portion to move from the cylindrical shape to a deformed shape when pressing the distal end of the tip portion against an external object such that the distal end of the tip portion deflects from the external object. 2. The delivery apparatus of claim 1, wherein the tip portion comprises a single slit. 3. The delivery apparatus of claim 2, wherein the single slit extends around a partial circumference of the wall. 4. The delivery apparatus of any one of claims 2-3, wherein the tip portion comprises two openings that are respectively connected to two distal ends of the single slit, wherein the two openings have a larger diameter than a width of the single slit. 5. The delivery apparatus of any one of claims 2-4, wherein the single slit divides the tip portion into a proximal portion and a distal portion, wherein when pressing the distal end of the tip portion against the external object, the tip portion collapses at the single slit such that a segment of the distal portion overlaps a segment of the proximal portion, thereby moving the tip portion to the deformed shape. 6. The delivery apparatus of any one of claims 2-5, wherein after moving to the deformed shape, the tip portion contacts the external object at an outer surface of the wall located at circumferentially opposite side of the single slit. 7. The delivery apparatus of any one of claims 2-6, wherein the dock sleeve is configured to move to a helical configuration when the dock sleeve covering the docking device is moved out of a delivery sheath of the delivery apparatus, wherein when the dock sleeve is in the helical configuration, the single slit faces radially inwardly toward a central axis of helical turns formed by the dock sleeve. 8. The delivery apparatus of claim 1, wherein the at least one slit comprises one or more slits on transverse planes that are perpendicular to a longitudinal axis of the dock sleeve. 9. The delivery apparatus of claim 8, wherein each slit extends around a partial circumference of the wall. 10. The delivery apparatus of any one of claims 8-9, wherein the one or more slits are equally spaced from one another in an axial direction along the tip portion. 11. The delivery apparatus of any one of claims 8-10, wherein two ends of each slit are respectively connected to two openings on the tip portion, wherein the two openings have a larger diameter than a width of the slit adjacent the two ends. 12. The delivery apparatus of any one of claims 8-11, wherein each slit has a middle portion and two side portions located on opposite sides of the middle portion, wherein the two side portions are symmetric about the middle portion. 13. The delivery apparatus of claim 12, wherein when no compressive load is applied to the distal end of the tip portion, each slit is wider in the middle portion than the two side portions, wherein when pressing the distal end of the tip portion against the external object, gaps within the one or more slits are reduced by the wall surrounding the slits, thereby moving the tip portion to the deformed shape. 14. The delivery apparatus of any one of claims 8-13, wherein after moving to the deformed shape, the tip portion contacts the external object at an outer surface of the wall located at circumferentially opposite side of the one or more slits. 15. The delivery apparatus of claim 1, wherein the at least one slit comprises a plurality of slits extending axially along the wall of the tip portion. 16. The delivery apparatus of claim 15, wherein the plurality of slits is equidistantly spaced along a circumference of the tip portion. 17. The delivery apparatus of any one of claims 15-16, wherein the wall of the tip portion forms a plurality of flaps between the plurality of slits, wherein when pressing the distal end of the tip portion against the external object, the plurality of flaps extends radially outwardly, thereby moving the tip portion to the deformed shape. 18. A dock sleeve for implanting a docking device at a native valve, the dock sleeve comprising: a body portion configured to retain the docking device; and a tip portion connected to a distal end of the body portion, wherein the tip portion comprises a wall and a slit cutting through a thickness of the wall, wherein the slit divides the tip portion into a proximal portion and a distal portion, wherein the tip portion has a cylindrical shape when no compressive load is applied to a distal end of the tip portion, and wherein when pressing the distal end of the tip portion against an external object, the tip portion collapses at the slit such that a segment of the distal portion overlaps a segment of the proximal portion, thereby causing the distal end of the tip portion to deflect from the external object. 19. A dock sleeve for implanting a docking device at a native valve, the dock sleeve comprising: a body portion configured to retain the docking device; and 

 a tip portion connected to a distal end of the body portion, wherein the tip portion comprises a wall and one or more slits cutting through a thickness of the wall, wherein the tip portion has a cylindrical shape when no compressive load is applied to a distal end of the tip portion, and wherein when pressing the distal end of the tip portion against an external object, the tip portion collapses at the one or more slits so as to bend the tip portion, thereby causing the distal end of the tip portion to deflect from the external object. 20. A dock sleeve for implanting a docking device at a native valve, the dock sleeve comprising: a body portion configured to retain the docking device; and a tip portion connected to a distal end of the body portion, wherein the tip portion comprises a wall and a plurality of longitudinal slits cutting through a thickness of the wall and dividing the wall into a plurality of flaps, wherein the tip portion has a cylindrical shape when no compressive load is applied to a distal end of the tip portion, and wherein when pressing the distal end of the tip portion against an external object, the plurality of flaps extends radially outwardly, thereby causing the distal end of the tip portion to deflect from the external object.
